chunks.text: Using Physics Simulators to Aid in Real-Time Robot Planning | EECS at UC Berkeley Using Physics Simulators to Aid in Real-Time Robot Planning Michael Tong EECS Department, University of California, Berkeley Technical Report No. UCB/EECS-2018-68 May 1

rkeley Technical Report No. UCB/EECS-2018-68 May 15, 2018 http://www2.eecs.berkeley.edu/Pubs/TechRpts/2018/EECS-2018-68.pdf In this report we focus on building a framework for a feedback loop for robot planning that incorporates physics simu

for robot planning that incorporates physics simulations in modeling complex interactions between the robots and their corresponding environment. By using a physics simulator to handle the dynamics, one can consider the tasks of building an environm

one can consider the tasks of building an environment and the dynamics of the robot separately, making it much easier to design and implement tests. Additionally, using a physics simulation allows for the testing of hypothetical environments and scen

the testing of hypothetical environments and scenarios that would be prohibitively expensive to physically build and test. This report will show how to conceptually build such a framework, describe in detail the realization of such a framework, and

n detail the realization of such a framework, and discuss the results of experiments performed using the framework. Advisors: Ronald S. Fearing BibTeX citation: @mastersthesis{Tong:EECS-2018-68, Author= {Tong, Michael}, Title= {Using Physics
